mainMenu.accountSettingsDescription: Review your subscription tier and update the email address and language used by the app.
mainMenu.accountSettingsFootnote: More account tools can be added here later.
mainMenu.summaryLoading: Loading your current device status and live power data.
mainMenu.summaryUnavailable: Live dashboard data is currently unavailable. Check the local API connection and token configuration.
mainMenu.summaryStatsUnavailable: Device inventory shows {onlineCount} of {totalCount} devices online. Live power stats are currently unavailable.
mainMenu.summaryDevicesUnavailable: {consumptionKw} kW is currently managed across your power limits, with {productionKw} kW of production available right now.
mainMenu.summaryProductionAhead: {onlineCount} of {totalCount} devices are online. Solar production is ahead by {netKw} kW against {consumptionKw} kW of current consumption.
mainMenu.summaryConsumptionAhead: {onlineCount} of {totalCount} devices are online. Current consumption is {consumptionKw} kW, which is {netKw} kW above solar production.

sitesView.title: Sites
sitesView.description: Manage physical sites, timezone settings, and weather places used by other automation views.

electricityContractsView.title: Electricity Contracts
electricityContractsView.description: Create and update energy or transfer contracts used by controls and reporting.
electricityContractsView.name: Contract name

accountSettings.title: Account Settings
accountSettings.headline: Personal settings
accountSettings.description: Manage your account profile, language, and password from the hybrid app.
accountSettings.tierDescription: Your plan controls account limits and available automation capacity.
accountSettings.notifyPowerLimitExceeded: Notify when power limit is exceeded
accountSettings.passwordRequirements: Minimum 8 characters with letters, numbers and at least one uppercase letter.
accountSettings.passwordInvalid: Password must be at least 8 characters and contain letters, numbers and at least one uppercase letter.
accountSettings.availableNowDescription: This view currently supports account tier details, email updates, and language selection.

devices.title: Your Infrastructure
devices.description: Manage and monitor your precision energy grid components. Real-time status for every connected unit across your network.
devices.failedLoadHeatPumpState: Failed to load heat pump state
devices.failedSendHeatPumpCommand: Failed to send heat pump command
devices.provisionDescription: Quickly add a new device to your energy ecosystem
devices.relayDescription: Toggle relay outputs in real time for this MQTT-connected standard device.
devices.heatPumpTestDescription: Load the current state, edit the command payload, and send it directly to this heat pump.
devices.heatPumpStateHelp: Refresh the live state, use the current or last polled payload, edit it if needed, and send the final command directly to the device.
devices.mitsubishiEditorHelp: Changes mark the edited Mitsubishi fields as effective so the command updates only the selected settings.

controls.title: Your Controls
controls.description: Manage price limits, cheapest-hour schedules, manual overrides, and shared control resources.
controls.createControlDescription: Add price and schedule logic for connected loads

createAccount.title: Create Porssiohjain Account
createAccount.description:
This will create a new account for you automatically.
A unique UUID and Secret will be generated.
Save them carefully. They will only be shown once.
createAccount.saveWarning: Copy your UUID and Secret now. They will not be shown again after you leave this page.

powerLimits.title: Power Limits
powerLimits.description: Keep total consumption under configured kilowatt limits.
powerLimits.label: Power Limit #{id}
powerLimits.unlinkDescription: This unlinks the device from this power limit.
powerLimits.deleteDescription: This removes the power limit and its linked device rules.

productionSources.title: Own Production
productionSources.description: Track local generation and switch loads by production level.
productionSources.label: Production Source #{id}
productionSources.failedRemoveHeatPumpRule: Failed to remove heat pump rule
productionSources.failedUpdateHeatPumpRule: Failed to update heat pump rule
productionSources.failedLoadHeatPumpState: Failed to load heat pump state
productionSources.removeRuleDescription: This removes the device rule from this production source.
productionSources.removeHeatPumpRuleDescription: This removes the heat pump rule from this production source.
productionSources.statePlaceholder: Paste or edit the heat pump state payload here.
productionSources.heatPumpRuleHelp: Query the latest device state, adjust it, then save the target action for this production threshold.
productionSources.heatPumpStateHelp: Choose the current or last polled state as a base, then modify the payload before saving it into the rule.
productionSources.invalidMitsubishiState: The current Mitsubishi state payload is not valid JSON.
productionSources.mitsubishiEditorHelp: For Mitsubishi devices you can edit power, operation mode, target temperature, and fan speed directly. The JSON payload is updated automatically.
productionSources.deleteDescription: This removes the production source and its linked device rules.

weatherControls.title: Weather Controls
weatherControls.description: Switch devices from site weather thresholds.

addDeviceType.title: Add Device
addDeviceType.missingDeviceDescription: Our engineering team is constantly adding new hardware drivers.

deviceTypes.shelly-pro-relays.title: Shelly Pro Relays
deviceTypes.shelly-pro-relays.description: Devices switching on loads like Pro 1/1PM, Pro 2PM, Pro 3, and Pro 4PM.

deviceTypes.toshiba-heat-pump.description: Devices using the Toshiba Home AC Control app.

addDeviceConfigure.title: Add Device
addDeviceConfigure.deviceNameHelp: Choose a name that identifies the location or function
addDeviceConfigure.timezoneHelp: Defaults to your current timezone and supports quick search
addDeviceConfigure.invalidTimezone: Select a valid timezone from the available list.
addDeviceConfigure.chooseAcDescription: Load devices from your vendor account and choose the indoor unit to connect.
addDeviceConfigure.credentialsRequired: Enter your heat pump app username and password before selecting an AC device.
addDeviceConfigure.chooseReturnedDevice: Choose the device returned by your {deviceType} account.
addDeviceConfigure.noSelectableDevices: No selectable AC devices were returned for these credentials.

addDeviceReview.title: Add Device
addDeviceReview.headline: Provision device in Energy Controller
addDeviceReview.description: Confirm the selected device details and provision the device in your Energy Controller service.

addDeviceIntegration.title: Add Device
addDeviceIntegration.headline: {deviceType} integration
addDeviceIntegration.description: Your device has been provisioned. If your device is a Shelly device, add the script below to the device's executable {} scripts in the Shelly application.
addDeviceIntegration.instructions: Open Shelly Smart Control, go to your configured Shelly device, add the required script, then paste the generated content below. This page is intentionally separate so other device types can later show their own post-provisioning instructions.

addControl.title: Add Control
addControl.headline: Create Control
addControl.description: Define the price thresholds, daily run target, timezone, and operating mode used by the control scheduler.
addControl.invalidTimezone: Select a valid timezone from the available list.

charts.failedNordpool: Failed to load today's Nord Pool pricing. {error}
charts.noNordpool: Today's Nord Pool pricing is not available yet.

charts.noProductionHistory: No production history is available for the last 24 hours.
charts.productionDescription: Last 24 hours of averaged production in {timezone}.

notifications.title: Control notifications
notifications.description: Send notification emails when this control is active within the selected time window.
notifications.name: Name
notifications.cheapestHelp: Use `0` cheapest hours to match any active hour.

manageDevice.label: Device #{id}

manageControl.failedLoadHeatPumpState: Failed to load heat pump state
manageControl.failedUpdateHeatPumpRule: Failed to update heat pump rule
manageControl.failedRemoveHeatPump: Failed to remove linked heat pump
manageControl.label: Control #{id}
manageControl.description: Tune scheduler thresholds, mode selection, and override behavior for this control.
manageControl.noStandardLinks: No standard devices linked to this control yet.
manageControl.unlinkDeviceDescription: This unlinks the device from this control.
manageControl.alreadyLinked: This device channel is already linked to the control.
manageControl.noHeatPumpLinks: No heat pump devices linked to this control yet.

manageControl.invalidMitsubishiState: The current Mitsubishi state payload is not valid JSON.
manageControl.mitsubishiEditorHelp: For Mitsubishi devices you can edit power, operation mode, target temperature, and fan speed directly. The JSON payload is updated automatically.

manageWeatherControl.failedRemoveHeatPumpRule: Failed to remove heat pump rule
manageWeatherControl.failedLoadHeatPumpState: Failed to load heat pump state
manageWeatherControl.label: Weather Control #{id}
manageWeatherControl.description: Edit the site and manage weather-based standard or heat pump rules.

manageWeatherControl.removeHeatPumpRuleDescription: This removes the heat pump rule from this weather control.
manageWeatherControl.statePlaceholder: State will be loaded here. You can also paste or edit it manually.
manageWeatherControl.heatPumpRuleHelp: Use the selected heat pump state as the command to send when this weather condition matches.

manageWeatherControl.heatPumpStateHelp: Toshiba devices use hex state. Mitsubishi devices use JSON state. You can edit the fetched value before saving it to the rule.
manageWeatherControl.invalidMitsubishiState: The current Mitsubishi state payload is not valid JSON.
manageWeatherControl.mitsubishiEditorHelp: For Mitsubishi devices you can edit power, operation mode, target temperature, and fan speed directly. The JSON payload is updated automatically.
